key_insight: Si doping and higher Al content jointly trigger local coordination loss in beta-(AlxGa1-x)2O3, which correlates with gamma-phase inclusion formation and defect-driven phase instability.
engineering_relevance: The work provides a nanoscale diagnostic for predicting structural reliability in ultrawide-bandgap oxides under composition and doping conditions relevant to device processing.
method: Coordination-sensitive atom probe tomography to measure local cation coordination and composition.
2D compositional mapping plus nearest-neighbor/radial distribution analysis to detect inhomogeneity and coordination collapse.
Correlative STEM and DFT to verify spatial coincidence and explain vacancy-mediated phase transformation.
evidence_signal: Gamma-phase inclusions appear only at 6-17% Al with heavy Si doping (10^17-10^20 cm^-3), and the abstract reports quantitative first-shell Ga coordination reduction consistent with cation deficiency.
watch_item: The coordination-loss signature is compelling but is demonstrated on a specific Si-doped beta-(AlxGa1-x)2O3 system, so broader generalization to other oxides will need additional validation.
paper_title: Coordination-Sensitive Nanoscale Analysis of Defect-Driven Phase Transformation in Si-Doped (AlXGa1-X)2O3
